subject: civic
topic_title: THE MEANING OF THE SEVEN CORE FREEDOMS OF UDHR
learning_objectives: Understand the concept of the meaning of the seven core freedoms of udhr

sections.heading: Overview
sections.content: The seven core freedoms of the UDHR are those major rights that all members of the United Nations organization must entrench in their constitution and must be benefited by their citizens. The seven core freedoms are freedoms that cannot be taken away from any individual even in emergency situations.

sections.heading: The seven core freedoms are;
sections.content:
The right to life
Freedom from torture.
Freedom from enslavement and forced labor
Right to protection from imprisonment for debt.
Freedom from retroactive laws.
Rjght to recognition of persons before the law.
Freedom of thought, conscience and religion.

sections.heading: Importance of fundamental human rights
sections.content:
It helps in preventing oppressive government and thereby limiting the powers of the rules.
It gives constitutional protection for those rights listed in it.
It enables citizens to seek redress for violation of their tights in the law court.
It encourages orderliness.
Peace and harmony exists in a society where people enjoy their rights.
The constitution is a reference point for the citizens to claim and enjoy their rights.
It encourages us to defend and fight for what is right or proper.
It accelerates development.
It helps citizens to have the knowledge of his/her rights
